message:
docs(security): policy-denial audit wiring matrix (ft-hg6io partial)

ft-h90rh landed the storage-side infra in 4595cc0e
(policy_denied_audit table + record_policy_denial_audit async method).
ft-hg6io is the wiring work: call every authorize deny path through
the new write, plus a policy.rs convenience wrapper, plus tests.

Scope-split to surface-only in this slot because:
- policy.rs has +81/-16 uncommitted from pane 2 (ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter
  refactor; PolicyEngine.rate_limiter field type change)
- mcp_tools.rs has +70 uncommitted from pane 2 (rate-limiter threading
  through WaGetText/WaSearch/WaSend/WaWorkflowRun + a 6x duplicated
  `let policy_rate_limiter = Arc::clone(...)` cluster at ~:1535,
  flagged in 810ad4c9 for pane 2 cleanup)
- The 45-min slot isn't enough to land the GateOutcome enum refactor +
  5-caller update + 7-direct-site wiring + tests, especially while
  racing pane 2 on the same two files.

Ship: docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md enumerating
every authorize call site in mcp_tools.rs (8 sites total — 1 helper
gating 5 tools, 7 direct), current deny handling, and audit-write
status. Produces a precise target list for the eventual wiring agent:
12 distinct deny paths to instrument.

Includes a sequenced wiring plan (policy.rs wrapper → GateOutcome
refactor → direct-site wiring → round-trip test) with a rationale for
splitting into 3 follow-up beads once ft-eu0no lands.

No code edits — pure docs addition. ft-hg6io stays OPEN with the
matrix attached in its close-reason when the wiring actually ships;
or can be closed partial now and the 3 sub-beads filed.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3b0gh","title":"[review][LOW] build_trigger_automaton panic message drops the underlying aho-corasick error","description":"commit 4219e912 added crates/frankenterm-core/src/pattern_trigger.rs:272-276:

    Some(
        builder
            .build(patterns)
            .expect("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile"),
    )

The .expect replaces the prior silent-empty-fallback with a loud panic — correct direction. But the panic message is a static string that DROPS the underlying aho-corasick BuildError, which typically encodes *which* pattern triggered the failure (e.g., pattern too long, invalid Unicode boundary in ascii-case-insensitive mode, DFA size limit).

A user hitting this panic in production sees:

    thread 'main' panicked at 'non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile'

…with no indication of *which* rule's pattern broke. Operators can't triage without attaching a debugger or running strings on the binary to figure out which pattern pack was active.

Fix (one-line change):

    .unwrap_or_else(|err| panic!("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}"))

OR use .map_err + .expect:

    .map_err(|err| format!("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}"))
    .expect(...)

Either preserves the panic invariant while surfacing the aho-corasick diagnostic.

Minor concern, not a correctness bug. Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23 while verifying ft-lfnwn fix.

Tag: review, pattern-trigger, diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:29.529272Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:21.788904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:21.788634Z","close_reason":"Completed: panic now includes mode and underlying aho-corasick BuildError","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2.  {"id":"ft-he0w7","title":"[fuzz] add native_events::decode_wire_event fuzz target for socket wire protocol","description":"Surface: crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:521 — fn decode_wire_event(line: &str) -> Result<Option<NativeEvent>, String>.   #![no_main]

  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 64_000 { return; }
      let Ok(line) = std::str::from_utf8(data) else { return; };
      // decode_wire_event is private. Expose via pub(crate) or a
      // test-only re-export, OR target the nearest public entry point
      // (event_stream::try_decode_line if it exists, otherwise add
      //  gated behind a 
      // feature).
      let _ = frankenterm_core::native_events::decode_wire_event_for_fuzz(line);
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/native_events_wire/ — one file per WireEvent variant with realistic ts/pane_id values, plus malformed-base64 PaneOutput, oversized data_b64 (>MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES to hit truncation), empty line, non-UTF-8-in-JSON-string, deeply-nested escape sequences.

 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`).  {"id":"ft-hyrav","title":"events::NotificationCooldown::check: insertion_order not refreshed on cooldown-expired send, LRU evicts actively-used keys","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:1015-1064 NotificationCooldown::check has a subtle LRU-staleness bug: when a key's cooldown has EXPIRED and we emit Send (line 1036-1043), we update entry.last_notified to now and reset entry.suppressed_since_notify, but we do NOT move the key to the back of self.insertion_order. The key stays at its ORIGINAL insertion position. Next time the entries map hits max_capacity, the eviction path at line 1047-1050 pops insertion_order.pop_front() — which picks a key by ORIGINAL INSERTION ORDER, not by last-use. A busy key that's been 'refreshed' (send + reset) many times can be evicted while a truly-dormant key with a later insertion stays. Effect: the cooldown state is silently dropped for the recently-used key; subsequent check() sees no entry and treats it as a first occurrence, flooding out a notification that should have been suppressed or at least carried a 'suppressed_since_last' count. Same pattern already fixed in sibling EventDeduplicator at events.rs:867-870 (refresh insertion_order on window-expired reset). Fix: in the cooldown-expired Send branch, remove the key from its current insertion_order position and push_back — matching the EventDeduplicator pattern. Regression test: 3-capacity cooldown, insert A/B/C, advance time past cooldown + re-check A (causing expired Send + refresh), insert D (triggering eviction). Pre-fix A is evicted; post-fix B is evicted (truly oldest-by-use).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.607054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.222010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.221646Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36c6f7d3 (fix(events): refresh NotificationCooldown insertion_order on cooldown-expired send).  {"id":"ft-s9oci","title":"mux/domain follow-ups: blocking spawn_command in async, silent write! errors","description":"Deferred during /multi-pass-bug-hunting audit shipped as dcaf60ca. Three pass-1/2 findings were clean in domain.rs, but two design-level issues remain as follow-up work:

1. Blocking spawn inside async fn. LocalDomain::spawn_pane (domain.rs:667) calls pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd) which on Unix is a synchronous fork+exec. If the child binary or an interposed fork hook hangs, the async task's executor thread is blocked indefinitely. Fix direction: route the spawn through tokio::task::spawn_blocking (or the asupersync equivalent) and await the join handle. Scope: also audit pair.master.take_writer() on the same path.

2. Silently swallowed write! errors. LocalDomain::spawn_pane on the child-spawn-failure branch (domain.rs:693) renders the error into the new pane via write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok(). If the write itself fails the user sees a blank pane with no indication of the upstream spawn failure. Fix direction: log the nested error via tracing::warn! with both the original spawn err and the write err so the failure is at least visible in logs.

+{"id":"ft-siwlu","title":"[architecture] split read-path redaction out of policy.rs authorization surface","description":"Evidence:

 {"id":"ft-swa8k","title":"[SEV] workflows/runner.rs orphan-cx fallbacks break ambient-cx inheritance in 3 sites","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs lines 729, 1498, 2021 all use the stale fallback pattern:

  let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
  let injector_cx = cx.unwrap_or(&request_cx);

When the caller passes cx=None but an ambient Cx is present (Cx::current() is Some), this falls back to a brand-new for_request() cx instead of inheriting the ambient one. That is the exact bug commit 02a599a9 (refactor(workflows): inherit ambient cx in pane injection wrappers) fixed for the four WorkflowContext::send_* entry points by switching to:

  let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Concrete consequences:
- Line 729 (run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup): set_decision_capture runs under an orphan cx, so a parent cancellation cannot propagate through the capture adapter.
- Line 1498 (run_workflow_inner step loop send_text branch): policy-gated send_text for legacy non-cx callers injects under an orphan cx; parent cancellation does not abort the injection or its audit write.
- Line 2021 (pub async fn run event loop child spawn): runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx is invoked with a fresh cx, orphaning the spawned workflow from the runtime's ambient cx — cancelling the ambient cx does NOT cancel the child workflow.

Real non-test callers hit these paths: mcp_tools.rs:2145 calls runner.run_workflow(...) from an MCP handler (MCP handlers run under an ambient request cx), so MCP-initiated workflows run to completion even if the MCP request is cancelled. The run() event loop at line 1955 is the non-cx sibling of run_with_cx (line 2154) and can be invoked from inside a with_cx block — children spawned there escape the ambient scope.

Fix: replace each 'let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();' with the ambient-inheriting pattern 'let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);'. No behaviour change when no ambient cx is present; closes the ambient-inheritance gap for every caller that already has one.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T22:07:15.266712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792231Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792041Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 3 stale for_request() fallbacks with Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request) in runner.rs (replay_capture setup, step-loop send_text, run() event-loop child spawn). cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes clean.  {"id":"ft-up6u2","title":"[security] mission + tx contract loaders lack size cap — OOM vector for hostile repo .ft/mission/*.json","description":"# Bug

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:257) and
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:141) both
use bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-read
size guard.

Pattern-pack loader has this exact guard (ft-05hfm, MAX_PACK_BYTES =
16 MiB). Mission files under `.ft/mission/*.json` have the same
trust property as patterns under `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json,toml}` —
a hostile repo can ship them, and they're loaded at MCP tool
invocation time.

# Attack

1. Hostile repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json` = 5 GiB of garbage.
2. Operator (or agent via wa.mission_state MCP) opens the project.
3. `wa.mission_state` → `mcp_load_mission_from_path` →
   `read_to_string` inhales 5 GiB into memory → watcher OOMs.

Same attack for `.ft/mission/tx-active.json` via wa.tx_show / wa.tx_run.

`resolve_workspace_scoped_path` (mcp_missions.rs:61) blocks
path-traversal but does NOT cap file size inside the workspace.

# Fix

Add `MAX_MISSION_BYTES` + `MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES` constants (16 MiB
each, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES) with a stat-before-read check + a
second-line check after read, mirroring the pattern-pack loader
structure.

Both constants at 16 MiB because:
- mission JSON + 100 assignments ≈ 50-100 KiB realistically
- tx contract + plan ≈ similar
- 16 MiB = 200x headroom

# Tests

Regression tests similar to `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml`:
  - `mcp_load_mission_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`
  - `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","created_by":"pane3-mission-cap","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408112Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3e2ae80c. Added MAX_MISSION_BYTES and MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES (both 16 MiB, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES from ft-05hfm) + shared read_capped_file helper + 2 regression tests (load_tx_contract_rejects_oversize_json, load_mission_rejects_oversize_json). Preserved historical robot.tx_not_found / robot.mission_not_found error codes on NotFound branch. Full verification blocked on ft-golgl (pre-existing compile errors in diagnostic.rs, outcome.rs, workflows/handlers.rs under --features mcp); changes reviewed against the ft-05hfm pattern shipping on HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","mission","security"]}

 {"id":"ft-v5do5","title":"scan_pipeline: per-category trigger count parity not pinned (only totals compared)","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_scan_pipeline.rs` has three chunked-vs-batch trigger parity properties:
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity (line 206)
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries (line 229)
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries_with_compression (line 255)

All three assert only `batch_total == chunked_total` via `total_matches`. None compare the per-category distribution (`TriggerScanResult.counts`).

A future refactor that miscounts one category and compensates in another — e.g., an off-by-one in overlap skip that drops an Error match while spuriously counting a Warning — would pass all three existing tests while quietly corrupting per-category analytics (rate-limit detection, error clustering, workflow routing).

# Fix

Add `chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity` proptest: assert `batch_output.triggers.counts == chunked_output.triggers.counts` (HashMap equality) across arbitrary chunk boundaries. Keeps the definitive-rescan invariant honest at the category level, not just the aggregate level.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect, just coverage thinness. Shipping as a feature/test strengthening rather than a bug fix because the current code path IS correct (flush re-scans with the same scan_counts used by batch, so per-category counts MUST match); the test just doesn't pin it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:02.169355Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:32:36.447073Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:32:36.446610Z","close_reason":"Landed in 59ba4ceb — two new proptests (chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity + *_with_compression) assert batch.counts == chunked.counts HashMap equality across arbitrary chunk boundaries, closing the total-only gap in the existing parity suite. +# Policy-denial audit wiring matrix
+
+**Bead:** ft-hg6io — wire `StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit`
+(infra landed in ft-h90rh / 4595cc0e) into every `PolicyEngine::authorize`
+call site so a denied or approval-gated action lands a row in
+`policy_denied_audit` in addition to the existing `tracing::warn!`
+emission from `mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation` (ft-6mmyp / 02988c16).
+
+## Every authorize deny path in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`
+
+Line numbers are drift-sensitive; re-grep `engine.authorize(&input)` /
+`policy_engine.authorize(&input)` before editing.
+
+| # | Call site (line) | Tool | Surface | Write? | Existing deny handling | Audit-write status |
+|---|------------------|------|---------|--------|------------------------|--------------------|
+| 1 | :194 (`mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation` helper) | `wa.tx_run`, `wa.tx_rollback`, `wa.mission_pause`, `wa.mission_resume`, `wa.mission_abort` | Mcp | ✓ | `tracing::warn!(target: "ft::security::policy", ...)` on both Deny and RequireApproval (ft-6mmyp) | **✗ missing** — helper returns `None` on Allow / `Some(err_envelope)` on Deny. No audit row. Fix: return a `GateOutcome` enum carrying a prebuilt `PolicyDeniedAuditRecord`, let callers (already in `runtime.block_on` scope) write it. |
+| 2 | :1216 | `wa.get_text` | Mux | read | `MCP_ERR_POLICY` envelope on Deny | **✗ missing** — read-path deny isn't strictly a security breach, but recording it matches the invariant the bead sets and gives operators fleet-wide "who attempted reads they couldn't perform" forensics. |
+| 3 | :1626 | `wa.search` | Mux | read | `MCP_ERR_POLICY` envelope on Deny | **✗ missing** — same rationale as :1216. |
+| 4 | :2184 | `wa.send` | Mux | ✓ | Routes through `PolicyGatedInjector::send_text` which internally audits to `audit_actions` via `record_audit_action_redacted` on the success path; deny in dry-run branch emits envelope without audit | **partial** — success path audits to `audit_actions` (different table); deny path (and dry-run deny) does NOT write to `policy_denied_audit`. |
+| 5 | :2435 | `wa.workflow_run` | Workflow | ✓ | Deny returns `MCP_ERR_POLICY` envelope; RequireApproval attaches to `ApprovalStore::attach_to_decision` and returns envelope | **partial** — has the deepest existing handling (approval-token issuance) but still doesn't record the deny itself. |
+| 6 | :3385 | `wa.reserve` | Swarm | ✓ | `MCP_ERR_POLICY` envelope on Deny | **✗ missing** |
+| 7 | :3536 | `wa.release` | Swarm | ✓ | `MCP_ERR_POLICY` envelope on Deny | **✗ missing** |
+| 8 | :3795 | `wa.accounts_refresh` | Swarm | ✓ | `MCP_ERR_POLICY` envelope on Deny | **✗ missing** |
+
+## Total deny-path surfaces that need audit writes
+
+- 7 direct `authorize` sites in `mcp_tools.rs` (rows 2-8 above)
+- 5 tools routed through `mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation` (row 1 above)
+
+= **12 distinct deny paths**.
+
+## Suggested wiring plan (sequence matters)
+
+1. **Land pane 2's ft-eu0no** first. policy.rs has an uncommitted +81/-16
+   diff refactoring `PolicyEngine.rate_limiter: RateLimiter` →
+   `SharedRateLimiter`. Any change to policy.rs today will either collide
+   or bundle their in-flight work.
+2. **Add the policy.rs convenience wrapper** once policy.rs is clean:
+   ```rust
+   pub fn build_policy_denial_record(
+       decision: &PolicyDecision,
+       tool_name: &str,
+       agent_id: Option<&str>,
+       intent_hash: Option<&str>,
+   ) -> Option<PolicyDeniedAuditRecord>
+   ```
+   Returns `None` for Allow, `Some` with the right `reason_code` /
+   `decision` constants (from `PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::REASON_CODE_*`) for
+   Deny / RequireApproval.
+3. **Refactor `mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation`** to return a `GateOutcome`
+   enum:
+   ```rust
+   enum GateOutcome {
+       Allow,
+       Denied { envelope: McpResult<Vec<Content>>, record: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord },
+       RequireApproval { envelope: McpResult<Vec<Content>>, record: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord },
+   }
+   ```
+   Each of the 5 callers (tx_run/rollback, mission_pause/resume/abort)
+   already lives inside `runtime.block_on(async { ... })`, so the deny
+   branch can `storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await.ok();`
+   (error-ignoring to match the existing `tracing::warn!` fire-and-forget
+   semantics).
+4. **Wire each of the 7 direct authorize sites** (rows 2-8) to also
+   build a `PolicyDeniedAuditRecord` on deny and write it. These are
+   already inside async blocks so no runtime plumbing needed.
+5. **Round-trip test** per-tool: seed a deny-by-default policy, invoke
+   the tool, `SELECT FROM policy_denied_audit WHERE tool_name = ?` and
+   assert the row shape.
+
+## Why not in this 45-min slot
+
+- Steps 2-4 require editing `policy.rs` + `mcp_tools.rs`, both contested.
+- Step 3 is the sharpest collision: the `GateOutcome` refactor changes
+  the helper signature, which ripples into the 5 callers in
+  `mcp_tools.rs` — pane 2 is also editing those 5 handlers.
+- Committing partial wiring under ft-hg6io would bundle pane 2's
+  uncommitted `build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter` threading
+  (~70 lines, including a 6× duplicated `let policy_rate_limiter = ...`
+  cluster at ~:1535 that's an apparent editor glitch). Flagged in
+  commit 810ad4c9 for pane 2 cleanup; doing the wiring on top would
+  compound the mess.
+
+## Follow-up beads to file once ft-eu0no lands
+
+- **ft-\<A\>**: policy.rs `build_policy_denial_record` helper + unit test
+- **ft-\<B\>**: refactor `mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation` to `GateOutcome` +
+  wire 5 mutation callers. Round-trip integration test for one
+  representative tool.
+- **ft-\<C\>**: wire the 7 direct authorize sites (rows 2-8). Per-site
+  deny-policy integration test.
+
+Splitting this way keeps each bead under ~200 LoC so the implementer
+can land them cleanly in sequence.
